mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and easing supply constraints, which improves profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during inventory accumulation phases or when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.3 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of high-inflation months or the signal fails to predict direction during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in > 50% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that translates into elevated corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 60-day returns remains below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that immediately threatens corporate output and cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy expectations.
